Module 1 returns a static string: `'hey, I am bar'`.\n  - Module 2 requires Module 1 and logs its output.\n  - Module 3 requires Module 2 and executes it, leading to the console log.\n- The overall flow is straightforward: main module triggers a chain of require and function calls, resulting in a console log of a static string.\n\n---\n\n### Review of the Reports:\n\n**Common Findings:**\n- All reports agree that the code is a standard module bundle with no obfuscated code, no dynamic execution, and no suspicious behavior.\n- No network activity, data leaks, or external data handling are present.\n- The code outputs static strings via console.log, with no indication of untrusted data or malicious intent.\n- The structure is typical for bundled JavaScript code.\n\n**Assessment of Scores:**\n- All reports assign:\n  -
